steps.title: Convert changes to multipliers
steps.content: An increase of $148\%$ means the new value is $100\%+148\%=248\%$ of the original, so multiply by $1+1.48=2.48$. A decrease of $14\%$ means the new value is $86\%$ of the previous value, so multiply by $1-0.14=0.86$.

steps.title: Apply the changes in order
steps.content:
The poster is first multiplied by $2.48$ from the end of $2014$ to the end of $2015$, then by $0.86$ from the end of $2015$ to the end of $2016$. The combined multiplier is
$$2.48\cdot0.86=2.1328$$

steps.title: Convert back to an increase
steps.content:
A final value of $2.1328$ times the original means the poster is $1.1328$ times the original value above where it started. Convert that excess multiplier to a percentage increase:
$$2.1328-1=1.1328=113.28\%$$
The net percentage increase is $113.28\%$. The answer is A.
